agent_output: {"integration_assessment":{"component_interactions":[{"from":"InventorySyncService","to":"ExternalInventoryAPI","status":"risk","issue":"No timeout configured on outbound HTTP call. A hung connection will block the sync indefinitely, potentially overlapping with the next 5-minute cron tick and causing cascading resource exhaustion."},{"from":"InventorySyncService","to":"LocalDatabase","status":"risk","issue":"If the external API returns corrupted or partial data (e.g., stock count of -1, or missing products), there is no validation gate before writing to the database. Silent data corruption risk."},{"from":"SchedulerCron","to":"InventorySyncService","status":"risk","issue":"No mutual exclusion. If a sync run takes longer than 5 minutes (especially with no timeout), the next scheduled run can overlap, leading to duplicate writes or database contention."}],"contract_compliance":"Unknown — no schema validation on the external API response is described. If the upstream API changes its contract, the service will silently write bad data or crash.","data_flow_integrity":"Unverified — no checksums, no row-count reconciliation, no diff logging. There is no way to detect whether a sync run partially applied or wrote stale data."},"observability_review":{"logging":{"coverage":"gaps","gaps":["Only two log lines exist: success and failure. No structured logging (JSON) — plain string concatenation makes log parsing and alerting nearly impossible.","No log of sync duration, number of records processed, or records changed.","No correlation ID or run ID to tie a sync cycle's logs together.","Error logging captures err.message only — stack traces, upstream HTTP status codes, and response bodies are lost.","No log differentiating 'zero records returned' (possible upstream issue) from 'all stock unchanged' (normal)."]},"metrics":{"coverage":"missing","gaps":["No sync_duration_seconds histogram — cannot detect slow degradation.","No sync_records_processed counter — cannot detect partial syncs.","No sync_errors_total counter — cannot alert on failure rate.","No sync_last_success_timestamp gauge — the single most important metric for a periodic job. Without it, silent failures are invisible.","No inventory_api_response_time histogram — cannot detect upstream latency issues before they cause timeouts."]},"tracing":{"coverage":"missing","gaps":["No distributed trace context propagated to the external API call.","No span instrumentation for DB write phase vs API fetch phase — cannot isolate where time is spent."]},"debugging_capability":"Severely limited. An on-call engineer at 3am would see either 'sync completed' or 'sync failed: <message>' in logs. They would have no way to determine: how many records were affected, how long the sync took, what the upstream API returned, whether the DB write succeeded partially, or whether runs are overlapping. This is not debuggable in production."},"resilience_assessment":{"failure_modes":[{"scenario":"External inventory API is down","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Add retry with exponential backoff (3 attempts, base 2s, jitter). Log each retry attempt. After final failure, emit a sync_errors_total metric increment."},{"scenario":"External API responds but extremely slowly (hangs)","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Add a 30-second timeout on the outbound HTTP call. AbortController for fetch, or timeout option for axios. This is a MUST-HAVE — without it, a single hung connection can cascade into resource exhaustion."},{"scenario":"External API returns 200 but with empty or malformed payload","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Validate response schema before writing. If product count drops by >50% vs last sync, flag as anomalous and skip write (or require manual override). Prevents mass zero-stock events."},{"scenario":"Database write fails mid-batch","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Wrap the DB write in a transaction. On failure, roll back the entire batch and log the partial state. Emit metric."},{"scenario":"Overlapping sync runs due to previous run exceeding 5-minute interval","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Implement a mutex lock (in-memory flag or DB advisory lock). If the previous run is still active, skip the current tick and log a warning with metric."},{"scenario":"Service process crashes or OOMs during sync","handling":"partial","recommendation":"Since there are no migrations, re-deploy is safe. But add a liveness probe and memory limits to container spec so orchestrator can restart automatically."}],"retry_config":"None. Must add exponential backoff with jitter, 3 attempts max.","timeout_config":"None. CRITICAL gap — must add 30s timeout on outbound API call.","circuit_breakers":"None. Recommended for future iteration: after N consecutive failures, open circuit and emit alert. Acceptable to defer if retries and timeouts are added first.","graceful_degradation":"None. On any failure, stale stock data persists silently. At minimum, track staleness age and alert if last successful sync exceeds 15 minutes."},"operational_readiness":{"deployment_ready":false,"rollback_capable":true,"rollback_plan":"Re-deploy previous container image. No database migrations in this release, so rollback is clean. Verify post-rollback that the previous sync schedule resumes correctly.","monitoring_coverage":"Nonexistent. No metrics, no health endpoint, no alerting. The team will not know this service is broken until customers report incorrect stock levels.","on_call_documentation":"Missing. Requires a runbook covering: how to manually trigger a sync, how to verify sync freshness, how to check upstream API health, how to identify overlapping runs, and escalation contacts for the external inventory API provider.","new_infrastructure_required":{"new_env_vars":["INVENTORY_API_TIMEOUT_MS","INVENTORY_SYNC_RETRY_MAX","INVENTORY_SYNC_RETRY_BASE_DELAY_MS"],"new_services":[],"new_queues_topics":[],"config_changes":["Add /health endpoint route returning sync staleness age","Add metrics exporter endpoint (Prometheus /metrics or equivalent)","Add alerting rule: sync_last_success_timestamp older than 15 minutes"],"infra_provisioning":["Dashboard for sync metrics (duration, success rate, records processed)","Alert routing for sync failure notifications"]},"database_safety":{"has_migrations":false,"reversible":true,"data_preserving":true,"large_table_impact":false,"details":"No schema changes. Writes update existing stock count columns. Rollback is safe."},"verdict":"NOT READY"},"escalations":[{"type":"missing_observability","issue":"Zero metrics instrumentation and no health endpoint","reason":"Without sync_last_success_timestamp, sync_duration, and sync_errors_total metrics, this service can fail silently for hours. There is no way to detect, alert on, or debug failures. An on-call engineer would be blind. This is the highest priority blocker.","routed_to":"Coach K"},{"type":"infrastructure_gap","issue":"No timeout on outbound HTTP call to external inventory API","reason":"A hung upstream connection will block the sync process indefinitely. With a 5-minute cron, this can cascade into overlapping hung processes, resource exhaustion, and eventual OOM. This is a production safety issue, not a nice-to-have.","routed_to":"Coach K"},{"type":"integration_risk","issue":"No response validation or anomaly detection on upstream API data","reason":"If the external API returns an empty array, zeroed stock counts, or malformed data, the service will blindly overwrite the local database. This could cause mass out-of-stock events visible to customers. Need at minimum a sanity check on record count delta.","routed_to":"Coach K"}],"confidence":{"level":90,"high_confidence_areas":["Observability gaps are clear and critical","Timeout absence is an objective production safety issue","Rollback safety is straightforward given no migrations"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact resource exhaustion behavior depends on runtime (Node.js event loop vs thread pool) which was not specified","Database write atomicity depends on ORM/driver configuration not visible in description"],"assumptions":["Service runs on a container orchestrator (K8s or similar) with image-based deploys","The 5-minute schedule is cron-based or setInterval-based with no built-in overlap protection","console.log outputs are captured by a log aggregator but are not structured JSON"]}}
